title: Review the k6 run and capture baseline numbers

blocks.content:
You already ran the spike and wrote the UTC start time. This milestone is the analysis step: you treat the **Test Result** view like a short readiness review. You confirm what passed, pull the few numbers the rest of the path expects in your notes, and pause on what those results do not prove before you leave k6 for **Explore**.

To review the k6 run and capture baseline numbers, complete the following steps.

1. Open the **Test Result** view for the run you just finished.

   After the run, Grafana Cloud k6 usually lands you there automatically. If you navigated away, open **Testing & synthetics**, then **Performance**, then **Projects**, open your project, select this test, then select the run in the Test runs table. Refer to [Inspect test results](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/inspect-test-results/) for the full layout.

1. Skim **Performance** overview at the top.

   Use it for overall response-time shape and how virtual users ramp while the run was live. The project list also colors each run green when no thresholds failed and red when at least one threshold failed; refer to [Manage projects](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/projects-and-users/projects/#test-run-status) if you need context for status colors.

1. Open the **Thresholds** tab.

   Each row is a threshold from your script's `options.thresholds`. Green means that condition passed for the run; red means it failed. This lab includes a lenient p95 `http_req_duration` rule and a checks rule so you have something concrete to read. Refer to [Inspect thresholds](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/inspect-test-results/inspect-thresholds/) if the tab label in your stack reads **Threshold** instead of **Thresholds**.

1. Open the **Checks** tab.

   Confirm pass or fail for each `check()` in the script (here, HTTP status 200). Refer to [Inspect checks](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/inspect-test-results/inspect-checks/) for what the tab shows.

1. Open the **HTTP** tab (or request metrics in the lower section) if you need an explicit p95 `http_req_duration` value in milliseconds.

   Use this tab when the overview does not show the value clearly.

1. Fill the capture table from the **Test Result** views.

| Capture | Your value |
| --- | --- |
| Thresholds | pass / fail |
| Checks | pass / fail |
| p95 `http_req_duration` | ___ ms |
| Peak VU + minute offset | |
| Latency trend across run | |

| Reflection | Your answer (1–2 sentences) |
| --- | --- |
| If every threshold passed, does that prove production readiness? | |

Check your work: you can move on when the capture table is filled, you answered the reflection row, and the **Test Result** view matches a completed run. If a tab is empty or times look wrong, open the troubleshooting topics for this milestone.

You now have k6 timing, checks, thresholds, and a UTC window in your notes. The next milestone leaves Grafana Cloud k6 and opens **Explore** on your metrics data source. You reuse the same absolute time range to ask whether synthetic probes saw the same spike your VUs drove.
